title: Potential Hidden Agenda Identification
use_when: Potential Hidden Agenda Identification. Use when the user needs a product workflow for stakeholder management related to potential hidden agenda identification. Trigger terms: stakeholders, analysis, organizational-politics, negotiation, stakeholder-management.
do_not_use_when: Do not use Potential Hidden Agenda Identification when the request is mainly technical implementation, analytics modeling, or UX critique; route to the narrower Productize skill that owns that artifact instead. Do not use it when the user lacks enough context to distinguish facts, assumptions, and decision risk.
output_artifact: Potential Hidden Agenda Identification stakeholder narrative with audience, message, risks, asks, and follow-up owner
framework_fit: Potential Hidden Agenda Identification fits Stakeholder Communication work in the Align lifecycle when the user needs Potential Hidden Agenda Identification stakeholder narrative with audience, message, risks, asks, and follow-up owner and has enough product context to make tradeoffs explicit. Use it to produce a decision-ready artifact, not a framework tour.
failure_modes: Produces Potential Hidden Agenda Identification stakeholder narrative with audience, message, risks, asks, and follow-up owner without tying recommendations to the user's Align stage, evidence, and decision pressure.
Treats Potential Hidden Agenda Identification as generic Stakeholder Communication advice instead of naming assumptions, missing inputs, risks, and the next validation step.
Fails to route away from Potential Hidden Agenda Identification when another Productize skill owns the requested artifact more directly.

examples.prompt: Use $potential-hidden-agenda-identification to turn this potential context into a decision-ready Potential Hidden Agenda Identification stakeholder narrative with audience, message, risks, asks, and follow-up owner.
examples.expected_artifact: Potential Hidden Agenda Identification stakeholder narrative with audience, message, risks, asks, and follow-up owner
